topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments related to taxation, education, environmental management, military recognition, and public health initiatives.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the exclusion from gross income of the discharge of qualified principal residence indebtedness.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of projects to treat impaired surface water.
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award competitive matching grants to states.

Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an income-based tax credit for legal expenses paid to establish a legal guardianship of a disabled individual.
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for 25% of the cost of qualified nonroad equipment.

topic.name: U.S. Customs and Trade Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and amendments related to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States and enforcement of customs laws, particularly for textiles and imported goods.

Document 2: ensures that seizures, detentions, special operations, and Textile Product Verification Teams (TPVTs) remain the primary focus of their efforts to enforce U.S. customs laws

requires the seizure and forfeiture of an imported textile or apparel article for which a trade preference has been claimed in cases where the importer has either misdescribed or not verified the article's country of origin.
Document 3: amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to provide for the duty-free treatment of certain recreational performance outerwear.
requires the Secretary of the Treasury to impose and collect, with specified exceptions, a fee of 1.5% of the appraised value of imported recreational performance outerwear.

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving health care services and benefits for veterans.

income imputed from the discharge of student loan amounts

5: provides that if a VA medical facility is unable to provide services due to a major disaster
Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall contract with non-VA facilities

service on active duty in a theater of combat operations

8: eliminates the requirement limiting reimbursement for emergency treatment in a non-VA facility

topic.name: Oil and Gas Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures related to the exploration, production, and regulation of oil and gas resources.

Document 1: Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a new oil and gas leasing program under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) for a five-year period
Requires the Secretary to include each state-nominated area in the final program unless the impacts of oil and gas development in a particular area cannot be effectively mitigated.
Document 2: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
carrying out any Act or program to reduce the effects of greenhouse gas emissions on climate change.
Document 3: This bill prohibits the Department of the Interior from issuing new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act unless they have been renegotiated to require royalty payments
Rentals or royalties received by the United States under covered leases must be deposited in the Treasury and used for federal budget deficit reduction.
Document 4: Amends the Clean Air Act to repeal prohibitions against aggregating emissions from any oil or gas exploration or production well
Requires the Administrator to issue a final rule adding hydrogen sulfide to the list of hazardous air pollutants.
Document 5: Expresses the sense of Congress that the National Petroleum Reserve (NPR) in Alaska remains explicitly designated to provide oil and natural gas resources to the United States
It is national policy to actively advance oil and gas development within the NPR.
Document 6: Amends the Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Act of 2004 to include in the Alaska Natural Gas Transportation Project any pipeline segment that the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) finds could feasibly be incorporated
is proposed and constructed before construction of the entire system.
Document 7: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to direct the Secretary of the Interior to precondition a new oil or gas lease upon the lessee's certification that the lessee will use the best available technology for all operations under the lease
Empowers the Secretary to order suspension of operations and cancel the lease if such a certification is not forthcoming.
Document 8: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas unless the lessee certifies for each existing lease that the lessee has diligently developed the lands
Establishes a civil penalty for noncompliance with this Act.
Document 9: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to revise provisions concerning ultra-deepwater and unconventional natural gas and other petroleum resources

Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a program of awards to support the development, demonstration, and commercialization of innovative technologies to prevent, stop, or capture large-scale accidental discharges of oil.
Document 10: Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from devising or implementing a carbon tax
Defines 'carbon tax' to mean a tax, fee, or price on emissions, including carbon dioxide emissions generated by the burning of coal, natural gas, or oil.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. immigration laws and regulations, including worker eligibility, visa requirements, and border security measures.

Document 1: Prohibits federal funding for any construction project in the Northern Mariana Islands which has a cost in excess of $100,000, unless the workforce for such project is composed of at least 60% U.S. workers.
Document 2: Sets forth border security and enforcement provisions, including increases in Border Patrol and investigative personnel.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require DNA testing for an alien seeking a visa based on a biological relationship to a family member.
Document 4: Prohibit a state from issuing a driver's license or identification document to a person unless the state has complied with certain citizenship or lawful immigration status verification requirements.
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act with respect to V-visa nonimmigrants to include certain Haitian nationals whose petition for a family-sponsored immigrant visa was approved.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act regarding U-visa provisions to limit derivative visas to spouses and children of an alien victim.
Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exclude from the definition of 'unauthorized alien' any alien otherwise admitted to and lawfully present in the United States.
Document 8: Establish an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa (StartUp visa) for a sponsored alien entrepreneur.
Document 9: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act to repeal the denial of an unlawful alien's eligibility for higher education benefits.
Document 10: Establish a non-immigrant W-visa category for an alien who is the spouse or child of a U.S. national or citizen.

topic.name: EMP Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on monitoring and reporting foreign countries' programs related to electromagnetic pulse weapons.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation, management, and protection of wilderness areas and federal lands.

Document 1: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, as wilderness
Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take necessary measures in such wilderness to control fire, insects, and diseases.
Document 2: Designates specified Bureau of Land Management (BLM) and National Forest System lands in California as wilderness areas
Designates certain land in the Los Padres National Forest in California, comprising approximately 2,174 acres, as the Machesna Mountain Potential Wilderness Area.
Document 3: Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas to be managed as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System

Establishes the Boulder-White Cloud Management Area on federal lands in the Challis National Forest, the Sawtooth National Forest, SNRA, and Challis District of the BLM that are not designated as wilderness areas under this Act.
Document 5: Designates certain lands in Siskiyou National Forest as the 'Copper Salmon Wilderness'
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate specified segments of the North and South Forks of the Elk River in Oregon as wild or scenic rivers.
Document 7: Designates specified wilderness areas in New Mexico and administered by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Establishes certain BLM-managed lands in the county as the Organ Mountains and Desert Peaks National Conservation Areas.

awarding scholarships to students who agree to work as child and adolescent mental health service professionals
8: require the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to develop and implement an outreach and education strategy
reduce stigma associated with mental health conditions and substance abuse
9: authorize the Attorney General to make grants to states to develop veteran's treatment courts
programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems
10: authorize appropriations for diabetes prevention and treatment through Indian health facilities

topic.name: Prescription Drug Regulation

topic.name: Mortgage Foreclosure and Mitigation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at preventing foreclosures, facilitating mortgage modifications, and regulating mortgage servicing practices.

Document 1: Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 (RESPA) to require the mortgagee, upon default of a federally related mortgage loan...

Requires the person commencing the foreclosure to notify the mortgagor, in writing, not less than five days before any action is taken to commence foreclosure.
Document 4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to deny any construction of the prohibition against residential mortgage loan originator steering incentives...
Document 5: Directs the Federal Mortgage Insurance Corporation (FMIC) established under title II of this Act to take all steps necessary to dissolve and eliminate Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.

Document 6: Directs each mortgage servicer to establish a deed-for-lease program which shall permit an eligible mortgagor to enter into a deed in lieu of foreclosure agreement...
Document 7: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 to terminate the authority of the Secretary of the Treasury to provide new mortgage modification assistance under the Home Affordable Modification Program (HAMP)...
Document 8: Prohibits a covered mortgagee from initiating either a nonjudicial or a judicial foreclosure...
Requires a covered mortgagee to use its best efforts to suspend any foreclosure proceeding initiated before enactment of this Act.
Document 9: Amends the National Housing Act to remove the aggregate and insurance benefit limitations on the number of home equity conversion (reverse) mortgages for elderly homeowners...
Document 10: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 to authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to establish a Family Foreclosure Rescue Corporation...
Requires the Secretary, to the extent home mortgages are acquired, to implement a plan that seeks to maximize loan restructuring on a sustainable basis.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, restoration, and sustainable management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.

Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Authorizes the Secretary to provide assistance to any state, local, or territorial government agency for any unforeseen or disaster-related circumstance regarding coral reef ecosystems.
Document 4: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 5: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.

The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) and the Department of the Interior are authorized to undertake emergency actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or a coral reef ecosystem.
Document 6: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy, revised as necessary, to include discussions of climate change.
Document 7: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to give priority to community-based local action strategies when awarding certain grants for conservation projects.
Document 8: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 9: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds to appropriate projects.
The U.S. Coral Reef Task Force is authorized to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Document 10: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.

Document 6: require the Secretary of Energy to report to certain congressional committees the reasons for any delayed approval of an application for a loan guarantee

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at enhancing the protection of personally identifiable information and addressing data breaches.

Document 1: the General Services Administration to procure an antivirus product suitable for federal agencies
provide to individuals whose personally identifiable information may have been compromised

Document 6: prohibit a nongovernmental individual or entity from knowingly collecting geolocation information
requires an entity that provides geolocation information to provide notice
Document 7: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach
known to have resulted in the theft of or unlawful access to the individual's personally identifiable information

requires timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised

Document 10: provides criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach

topic.name: Tax Fraud and Identity Theft Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing tax fraud and identity theft through enhanced security measures, reporting requirements, and penalties for violations.

implement an identity theft tax fraud prevention program that provides for a unique personal identification number (PIN) on tax returns.
Document 2: require U.S. entities to obtain and have an employer identification number (EIN) assigned by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS)
The IRS may disclose to federal law enforcement officials taxpayer identify information, including an EIN and information from an application for an EIN.

the IRS must submit to Congress a feasibility study regarding a program under which a person who has filed an identity theft affidavit with the IRS may elect to prevent the processing of any federal tax return.

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at regulating and reforming the banking sector, particularly in relation to the separation of banking and securities activities, capital requirements, and systemic risk management.

Document 1: Prohibits an insured depository institution from being an affiliate of any broker or dealer, investment adviser, investment company, or any other person or entity engaged principally in the issue, flotation, underwriting, public sale, or distribution of stocks, bonds, debentures, notes, or other securities.
Amends the Banking Act of 1933 (Glass-Steagall Act) to expand its prohibition against the transaction of banking activities by securities firms.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA) to apply the bank share or asset acquisition requirements of the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to any company that is or would become an industrial bank holding company.
Prohibits an industrial bank from being controlled by a commercial firm, except in certain circumstances.
Document 3: Requires the Board, in deciding whether and how to regulate, to assess the costs and benefits of regulatory alternatives, including the alternative of not regulating, and choose the approach that maximizes net benefits.
Directs the Board to make publicly available on its website staff salaries and financial information.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.

Document 7: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 (EESA) with respect to the repayment of Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) assistance.

Document 8: Amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010 with respect to minimum leverage capital requirements and minimum risk-based capital requirements for depository institution holding companies.

Document 9: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act with respect to mandatory fee disclosures on automated teller machines (ATMs).

Document 10: Subjects bank holding companies with total consolidated assets of $250 billion or more to enhanced supervision and prudential standards.

Document 8: Amends the Endangered Species Act to prohibit considering climate change in species threat determinations.
Document 9: States that the construction of the Keystone XL pipeline shall not adversely affect the American burying beetle.
Document 10: Amends the Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp Act to increase the price of the stamp for conservation funding.

Document 3: Amends the Rural Electrification Act of 1936 to establish an Office of Rural Broadband Initiatives at the Department of Agriculture.
The Under Secretary is responsible for administering all rural broadband-related grant and loan programs.
Document 4: Amends the Energy Policy and Conservation Act to revise requirements for the sale by the Secretary of Energy of products from the Northeast Home Heating Oil Reserve.
Conditions any sale on a finding by the President that there is a severe energy supply interruption.
Document 5: Amends the Calfed Bay-Delta Authorization Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to establish a program to provide assistance to non-federal interests for stability maintenance and improvement on levees.
Prioritizes projects based on the extent to which the levees covered are necessary for Bureau water deliveries.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to convey all right, title, and interest in specified land to the American Falls Reservoir District No. 2.
Requires the District to pay fair market value for the land.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury and the Secretary of State to jointly submit to Congress an annual report that identifies each person that provides material or financial support to ISIL.
Amends the Arms Export Control Act to apply transaction prohibitions against countries supporting acts of international terrorism.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish a national cemetery in the Sarpy County, Nebraska, region.
Serves the needs of veterans and their families in Nebraska, Iowa, and Missouri.
Document 9: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require a state Medicaid plan to provide for state reimbursement of patient navigator services.
Includes within the definition of 'medical assistance' certain patient navigator services.
Document 10: States as the purpose of this Act the establishment of the Raritan Bay Stewardship Initiative to identify, protect, and enhance sites within the Raritan Bay ecosystem.
Establishes the Raritan Bay Stewardship Initiative Region within New York and New Jersey.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Reform
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative actions aimed at reforming government agencies, enhancing accountability, and improving public health and education systems.

Document 1: Prohibits the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) from filling any position...
enforcement of any provision of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
Document 2: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to reduce from 14 years to 7 years the term of office of the members of the Board of Governors
Requires the Speaker and the minority leader of the House of Representatives... to create jointly a list of five candidates
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to make chiropractic personnel eligible for appointment
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) and the Surgeon General to ensure that Doctors of Chiropractic are trained
Document 4: amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 by requiring the Department of Education (ED) to rank institutions of higher education
establishes consumer warning requirements for institutions with low enrollment or graduation rates
Document 5: Requires the head of each executive agency to submit to the President and Congress a report on the implementation of recommendations made by specified Government Accountability Office (GAO) reports
to eliminate, consolidate, streamline, or better coordinate government programs
Document 6: Requires federal agency Chief Financial Officers to include multilingual services accounting information in their mandatory annual reports
Requires the OMB to issue guidance that an agency Chief Financial Officer must follow
Document 7: Requires the District of Columbia, upon an individual's arrest, to give the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) information necessary to determine the individual's citizenship
Any District officer... who knowingly violates the notification or detention requirements under this Act shall pay a civil penalty
Document 8: amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for 50% of an individual's qualified infertility treatment expenses
subject to specified limits based on dollar amounts and the taxpayer's adjusted gross income
Document 9: Amends the Public Health Service Act to establish within the Office of the Secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) the position of National Diabetes Coordinator
develop and coordinate implementation of a national strategy to reduce the incidence, progression, and impact of diabetes
Document 10: Establishes the Federal Agency Program Realignment and Closure Commission to evaluate each executive agency and its programs
submit to the President and Congress a list of the agencies and programs it recommends eliminating or realigning

topic.name: Government Programs and Corporations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to the establishment and enhancement of government programs and corporations aimed at addressing specific community needs and services.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code
require the recapture in subpart F income
Document 2: authorizes counties to establish a Regional Development Corporation
limits the use of Corporation funds
Document 3: authorizes the creation of a Carbon Storage Research Corporation
collect assessments to support carbon capture technologies
Document 4: expands the authority of the Secretary of Defense
facilitate the travel of members of the Armed Forces
Document 5: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate any country
Document 6: makes permanent the authority of the Department of Veterans Affairs
transport individuals to and from VA facilities
Document 7: authorizes the Secretary of Education to make grants
establish educational programs for expectant and parenting students
Document 8: amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act
set forth land selection rights of the 13th Regional Corporation
Document 9: amends the Export Enhancement Act of 1988
provide small and medium-sized businesses with support
Document 10: amends federal provisions concerning nonprofit research and education corporations
facilitate the conduct of research or education at VA medical facilities

topic.name: Tax Policy and Social Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to tax policy, social welfare programs, and environmental conservation efforts.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide that advance payments of the earned income tax credit are not transferable or assignable
Prohibits the collection of a debt from a debtor's federal tax refund by means of a refund anticipation loan.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow tax credits through 2019 for investment in an offshore facility using wind to produce electricity.
Defines 'offshore facility' to mean any facility located in the inland navigable waters of the United States.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow until June 30, 2010: a first-time homebuyer tax credit for all purchasers of a principal residence.
A refundable tax credit, up to $3,000, for the costs of refinancing a principal residence.
Document 6: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a new refundable tax credit based on the number of their qualifying children under the age of three.
Advance payments of credit amounts shall be made to taxpayers each month.
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to change provisions that refer to married couples to make such provisions equally applicable to legally married same sex couples.

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures focused on land management, conservation, and the transfer of land for public use or environmental protection.

Document 1: Prohibits the appropriation of funds... for any VA alternative energy generation project unless the funds for such project have been specifically authorized by law.
Document 2: Designates certain Bureau of Land Management (BLM) land in Oregon as wilderness...
Requires any land or interest within the boundary of the Wilderness acquired by the United States to become part of such Wilderness.
Document 3: This bill declares that the approximately one-acre portion of specified land... shall not be subject to the use restriction imposed in the deed for the transferred land.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey, by sale, a specified parcel of National Forest System land...
Requires Canyons-SkiLink to pay to the Secretary an amount that is equal to at least the fair market value of such land.
Document 5: Directs the Forest Service to convey approximately 67 acres of certain Forest Service land... to be used for the conservation of wildlife or natural resources or for a public park.
Document 6: This bill revises and reauthorizes the Lake Tahoe Restoration Act for 10 fiscal years...
The bill mandates priority programs in the Basin, including programs for fire risk reduction and forest management.
Document 7: Withdraws approximately 512 acres of covered land in Arizona... from all forms of: (1) entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws; (2) location, entry, and patent under the mining laws.
Document 8: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept title to any additional number of acres of real property... if such real property is conveyed or otherwise transferred to the United States by or on behalf of the Tribe.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Idaho County in Idaho approximately 31 acres of land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) for use as a shooting range.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating to work through the International Maritime Organization to establish agreements... to ensure in the Arctic: (1) placement and maintenance of aids to navigation; (2) appropriate icebreaking escort, tug, and salvage capabilities.
